methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier, having raised €7M in seed funding alongside a €152M facility commitment. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known comparables: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to QuantumDiamonds. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for companies at the same stage, adjacent stages, or similar valuation tiers yielded zero datapoints meeting the minimum threshold of three comparable companies. Reliance on the full-sector median would introduce significant methodological bias given the wide dispersion in stage, capital structure, and business model across the universe of quantum computing companies.

The absence of a sufficiently granular comparable set reflects the nascent and heterogeneous nature of quantum computing commercialization. Without meeting the threshold for peer-median triangulation, valuation of QuantumDiamonds would require alternative methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis, venture capital method application, or milestone-based frameworks—paired with explicit documentation of key assumptions around technology validation, competitive positioning, and path to revenue. The €152M facility commitment warrants separate analysis as a signal of stakeholder conviction, though it does not substitute for revenue-based or comparable-company valuation.
